title: campanula glomerata f. polessica
intro: Campanula glomerata f. polessica, a subspecies of the bellflower family, is a perennial plant native to the Ukraine. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect against chronic diseases. Campanula glomerata f. polessica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with some herbalists also recommending its use in compresses or topical applications for its wound-healing properties.
